_meta.verificationNote: AI Generated: Indicative customer information risk register. All risk ratings, scenarios, and treatment plans are illustrative. Organisations must conduct their own risk assessments proportionate to their size, complexity, and data holdings.

title: Customer Information Risk Register
description: Register of key customer information risks for Malaysian financial service providers under BNM MCIPD, with inherent and residual risk ratings.

risks.name: Unauthorised Disclosure by Staff via Social Media
risks.description: Staff with access to customer information deliberately or inadvertently disclose customer data through web-based communication platforms or social media channels, including end-to-end encrypted platforms.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement web content filtering for all customer-data-handling roles; deploy DLP monitoring on outbound web traffic; restrict personal device use in call centres and data processing areas

risks.name: Bulk Customer Data Exfiltration by Insider
risks.description: A malicious insider systematically downloads large volumes of customer information through authorised system access, portable storage devices, or email for sale or personal gain.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement UEBA to detect anomalous download patterns; enforce USB whitelisting; deploy data watermarking for bulk exports; enhance exit interview procedures with data access audit

risks.name: OSP Customer Data Breach
risks.description: An outsourced service provider handling customer information suffers a data breach due to inadequate controls, compromising the FSP's customer information held by the OSP.
risks.treatmentPlan: Enhance OSP due diligence with security posture scoring; require SOC 2 Type II reports from high-risk OSPs; implement continuous OSP monitoring through security rating services

risks.name: Inadequate Data Disposal Leading to Information Recovery
risks.description: Customer information is not securely destroyed during disposal, allowing recovery of data from discarded paper documents, decommissioned hardware, or returned storage devices.
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy certified data wiping tools (NIST SP 800-88 compliant); conduct witnessed destruction events quarterly; implement tamper-proof collection containers at all branches

risks.name: Customer Data Breach via Phishing or Social Engineering
risks.description: Staff are deceived through phishing emails, vishing calls, or social engineering into disclosing customer information or granting system access to malicious actors.
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy advanced email threat protection; conduct quarterly phishing simulations; implement MFA for all customer-data-access systems; establish real-time phishing reporting channel

risks.name: Unauthorised Access Due to Stale Access Rights
risks.description: Former staff or staff who have changed roles retain access to customer information systems, enabling continued unauthorised viewing, modification, or download of customer data.
risks.treatmentPlan: Integrate HR system with IAM for automated deprovisioning; implement same-day access revocation on termination; conduct quarterly access recertification campaigns

risks.name: Physical Document Exposure or Theft
risks.description: Physical documents containing customer information are left unattended, stolen from premises, lost during transit, or accessed by unauthorised persons in filing rooms or workstations.
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce clear-desk policy with spot checks; install badge-access for all filing rooms; provide lockable storage at all workstations; implement tracked courier for document transit

risks.name: Non-compliance with Customer Consent Requirements
risks.description: Customer information is disclosed to third parties without obtaining valid informed consent, or consent records are incomplete, invalid, or not properly maintained.
risks.treatmentPlan: Deploy digital consent management platform with audit trail; implement consent validity checks at point of disclosure; conduct annual consent records audit

risks.name: Cross-Border Disclosure Without Adequate Safeguards
risks.description: Customer information is transmitted to overseas entities, group companies, or foreign regulators without adequate data protection safeguards, regulatory approval, or customer consent.
risks.threatSource: Internal - process failure or legal gap
risks.treatmentPlan: Establish cross-border data transfer register; implement jurisdiction adequacy assessments; require legal review for all new cross-border data flows; deploy encryption for all cross-border transmissions

risks.name: Delayed or Inadequate Breach Notification to BNM
risks.description: The FSP fails to notify BNM immediately upon discovering a qualifying breach, or the notification content is incomplete, leading to regulatory non-compliance and potential enforcement action.
risks.treatmentPlan: Pre-draft BNM notification templates; establish designated officer escalation hotline; conduct annual breach notification drill; implement automated breach classification against notification triggers

risks.name: OSP Personnel Mishandling of Customer Information
risks.description: OSP personnel who have not been adequately vetted or trained mishandle customer information through negligence or deliberate misconduct, causing a breach.
risks.treatmentPlan: Require evidence of OSP personnel vetting before granting access; provide FSP-delivered training to key OSP personnel; conduct unannounced OSP site visits

risks.name: External Vendor Personnel Data Theft on FSP Premises
risks.description: External vendor personnel working on FSP premises (security guards, cleaners, maintenance engineers) access or steal customer information due to inadequate vetting and access restrictions.
risks.treatmentPlan: Require documented background checks for all on-premises vendor personnel; restrict vendor access to non-sensitive areas; escort vendors in sensitive zones; implement privacy screens on monitors

risks.name: Staff Photographing Customer Data from Screens or Documents
risks.description: Staff use personal mobile phones or cameras to photograph customer information displayed on screens or in physical documents, bypassing electronic data loss prevention controls.
risks.treatmentPlan: Restrict personal electronic devices in data-sensitive areas; install CCTV covering customer data processing areas; implement privacy screen filters; enforce clean-desk and screen-lock policies

risks.name: Insufficient Board Oversight of Customer Information Protection
risks.description: The Board fails to exercise adequate oversight, does not review management reports, or does not obtain annual written assurance on the effectiveness of customer information controls.
risks.treatmentPlan: Establish customer information protection as standing Board agenda item; implement structured quarterly reporting template; formalise annual assurance process with designated officer sign-off

risks.name: Customer Data Exposure Through Call Recording Systems
risks.description: Call recordings containing sensitive customer information are accessed by staff without a legitimate need-to-know, or call recording systems have inadequate access controls enabling bulk retrieval.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement granular RBAC on call recording platform; enable audit logging for all access events; deploy automated alerts for bulk playback requests; implement auto-expiry of recording access

risks.name: Data Breach at Outsourced Destruction Provider
risks.description: An outsourced service provider engaged for data destruction fails to properly destroy customer information, or customer data is exposed during transit to the destruction facility.
risks.treatmentPlan: Pre-shred paper documents before collection; use tamper-proof sealed containers; conduct quarterly unannounced audits of destruction provider; require destruction certificates with photographic evidence

risks.name: Inadequate Training Leading to Accidental Disclosure
risks.description: Staff who are inadequately trained on customer information handling procedures accidentally disclose customer data through misdirected emails, faxes, or sharing with wrong recipients.
risks.treatmentPlan: Implement email DLP rules to flag external sends of customer data; deploy autocomplete address book restrictions; enhance onboarding training with practical scenarios; measure training effectiveness quarterly

risks.name: Failure to Complete Breach Investigation Within Three Months
risks.description: Breach investigations are not completed within the required three-month timeframe due to resource constraints, investigation complexity, or poor case management, delaying Board reporting and BNM submission.
risks.treatmentPlan: Establish dedicated breach investigation team; deploy case management system with milestone tracking; engage standby forensic investigation partners; implement three-month countdown alerts

risks.name: Loss of Portable Storage Device Containing Customer Data
risks.description: An FSP-provided portable storage device (USB drive, external hard disk, laptop) containing customer information is lost or stolen, with data potentially accessible if not adequately encrypted.
risks.treatmentPlan: Enforce full-disk encryption on all portable devices; implement remote wipe capability; deploy hardware-encrypted USB drives only; maintain asset register with location tracking

risks.name: Independent Review Identifies Systemic Control Deficiencies
risks.description: The biennial independent review reveals systemic deficiencies in the FSP's customer information protection controls that have gone undetected, indicating failure in the first and second lines of defence.
risks.treatmentPlan: Strengthen first-line control self-assessment programme; implement continuous monitoring between independent reviews; establish rapid remediation process for independent review findings; track finding closure rates
